title: Stored web push endpoint can drive service-role reminder cron SSRF

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: endpoint is arbitrary text with no scheme/host allowlist or push-service constraint

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: owner_all RLS lets an authenticated user write their own subscription rows

affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper
affected_locations.detail: service-role cron selects all stored endpoints

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: stored endpoint is placed in pushSub and passed to webPush.sendNotification

attacker_controlled_source: Authenticated user-controlled row in public.web_push_subscriptions, directly writable through Supabase Data API under owner_all RLS or through the normal PWA subscription flow.
vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control: No endpoint URL scheme/host/IP validation before a privileged scheduled function sends to the stored endpoint with webPush.sendNotification.
impact: Authenticated SSRF/callback abuse and egress to attacker-chosen or internal HTTPS destinations from the Supabase Edge Function environment; also possible outbound request amplification until invalid endpoints are pruned.
plausibility: The table accepts endpoint as text and RLS checks only user_id ownership. web-push-reminders reads all rows with the service-role client and passes sub.endpoint to web-push without repository-visible allowlisting of known browser push services.
closest_apparent_control: Unique endpoint and stale-endpoint pruning handle duplication/lifecycle, not destination trust. Browser PushManager constrains the normal client path, but direct authenticated table writes bypass that client-side origin.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: Source table and owner RLS at migration lines 15 and 43-46; service-role select at web-push-reminders line 137; network sink at lines 220 and 249.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: Dynamic confirmation depends on web-push library endpoint parsing and runtime egress policy.

candidate_local_attack_path.entry: insert or upsert own web_push_subscriptions row with chosen endpoint and valid-looking key material
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: web-push-reminders cron runs and the user's habit reminder is due
candidate_local_attack_path.security_impact: server-side outbound request to attacker-selected endpoint from privileged scheduled worker

title: Stored web push endpoint can drive service-role coach-push SSRF

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: endpoint is arbitrary text with no scheme/host allowlist or push-service constraint

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: owner_all RLS lets an authenticated user write their own subscription rows

affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper
affected_locations.detail: coach-push service-role cron selects all stored endpoints

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: stored endpoint is passed to webPush.sendNotification

attacker_controlled_source: Authenticated user-controlled public.web_push_subscriptions row that coach-push later reads under service-role privileges.
vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control: coach-push reuses stored endpoint values without destination validation before sending notifications.
impact: Authenticated SSRF/callback abuse from the coach-push scheduled Edge Function; reachable independently from the regular reminder sender when coach_push is enabled and signal preconditions are met.
plausibility: coach-push selects endpoint, p256dh, auth, timezone from all subscriptions and sends to each sub.endpoint. The storage policy constrains row ownership but not endpoint destination.
closest_apparent_control: Feature flag, cron secret, eligible signal windows, and VAPID setup gate whether the worker runs; none validate the stored endpoint destination once a row is processed.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: Stored endpoint root at migration line 15; owner write policy at lines 43-46; service-role select at coach-push line 229; sendNotification sink at lines 350-352.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: Dynamic exploitability depends on web-push library URL restrictions and production egress policy.

candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: coach_push feature flag enabled, user has eligible signal and active subscription row, scheduled worker runs
candidate_local_attack_path.security_impact: server-side outbound request to attacker-selected endpoint from privileged coach-push worker

title: Direct support-email invocation bypasses feedback length/rating limits before Resend send

affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper
affected_locations.detail: documented callable Edge Function for app feedback email

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: function trims message and checks only non-empty, without server-side max length, rating range, or rate limit

affected_locations.label: closest_control
affected_locations.detail: client-side feedback validation limits message to 2000 chars and rating 1-5, but it is not enforced by the email function

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: unbounded message is sent to Resend email API

attacker_controlled_source: Authenticated user-controlled function body sent directly to support-email.
vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control: The Edge Function does not duplicate feedback length/rating/rate controls before sending email through Resend.
impact: Authenticated users can bypass client and feedback_reports database constraints to send oversized or repeated emails to the support inbox, consuming provider quota and creating operational spam/DoS.
plausibility: The normal client validates and inserts into feedback_reports first, but the function is independently invokable with a JWT and only checks for a non-empty message before constructing and sending HTML email.
closest_apparent_control: JWT authentication prevents anonymous abuse; client-side validation and database constraints protect the normal feedback table path only, not direct function invocation.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: Client validation enforces 10-2000 chars at lib/auth/validation.ts lines 20-27; support-email only checks non-empty at lines 74-81 and calls Resend at line 96.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: Runtime rate limits from Supabase platform or Resend account configuration are not visible in repository.

candidate_local_attack_path.entry: invoke support-email directly with large/repeated message body
candidate_local_attack_path.security_impact: email quota consumption and support inbox flooding

title: Habit routine AI endpoint forwards unbounded answers object to Gemini

affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper
affected_locations.detail: client invokes habit-routine with answers and localRecommendations

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: localRecommendations are sanitized and bounded, but answers has no equivalent sanitizer

affected_locations.label: closest_control
affected_locations.detail: Pro access and per-call AI quota are enforced before the model call

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: body.answers is embedded in the Gemini prompt payload

attacker_controlled_source: Authenticated Pro user's request body field `answers`.
vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control: No server-side schema, depth, field, or byte-size limit for `answers` before JSON.stringify and Gemini API submission.
impact: A Pro authenticated caller can inflate prompt input size, latency, and provider cost beyond intended wizard fields while consuming only one per-call quota unit.
plausibility: The endpoint deliberately sanitizes `localRecommendations` but forwards `body.answers` raw into the model request. Quota limits call count, not input token volume.
closest_apparent_control: JWT auth, Pro access, and AI quota reduce reachability, but do not bound input size or structure for the provider sink.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: Client sends answers in routine-ai lines 58-63; endpoint sanitizes recommendations at lines 143-189; quota at 207; sink includes answers at line 248.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: Exact provider token/cost impact requires runtime request-size limits from Supabase/Gemini not present in repository.

candidate_local_attack_path.entry: invoke habit-routine with oversized nested answers object and minimally valid localRecommendations
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: Gemini key configured and quota available
candidate_local_attack_path.security_impact: AI provider quota/cost and function latency/resource abuse

title: Unbounded web push subscriptions can amplify scheduled push worker fanout

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: subscription table has unique endpoint but no per-user row limit or endpoint format constraint

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: owner_all RLS permits authenticated users to manage all of their subscription rows

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: reminder worker selects all subscriptions and sends/logs per due subscription

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: coach-push groups all subscriptions by user and sends to every endpoint for that user

attacker_controlled_source: Authenticated user's ability to create many distinct web_push_subscriptions endpoints under their own user_id.
vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control: No repository-visible per-user subscription cap, endpoint format check, or server-side enrollment API that verifies subscriptions were created by the browser PushManager.
impact: Authenticated users can increase scheduled worker database reads, per-row processing, outbound push attempts, and provider/API cost. This can degrade cron processing and cause missed notifications for other users if batch/runtime limits are hit.
plausibility: The table only makes endpoint globally unique. Both scheduled workers read subscription rows and iterate endpoints. Invalid endpoints are pruned only after a send attempt; valid attacker-created endpoints can persist.
closest_apparent_control: Unique endpoint and 404/410 pruning limit exact duplicates and stale endpoints, but do not cap rows per user or prevent many syntactically distinct endpoints.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: Table definition has no per-user cap at migration lines 12-24; owner_all write policy at lines 43-46; scheduled fanout loops in web-push-reminders and coach-push.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: Operational impact depends on Supabase project quotas, cron timeout, and web-push service behavior.

candidate_local_attack_path.entry: insert many own subscription rows with unique endpoints
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: scheduled push workers enabled and user has due reminders or coach signal
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: global scheduled push loops and webPush.sendNotification
candidate_local_attack_path.security_impact: resource amplification and notification pipeline degradation
